title: InferenceQualityRubric
description: Defines per-axis scoring weights, pass thresholds, and aggregate pass policy for inference quality evaluation. Fixed by ADR-0153.

rubric_version.description: Semantic version string for this rubric definition (e.g. '1.0.0').

axes.description: Ordered list of scoring axes. Exactly three axes are required: correctness, format, latency.

name.description: Axis identifier. One of: correctness (factual/semantic accuracy), format (schema/template compliance), latency (response time in ms).

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what this axis measures.

weight.description: Relative weight of this axis in the aggregate score. All axis weights MUST sum to 1.0.

threshold.description: Per-axis pass threshold. For correctness and format: float 0.0-1.0. For latency: maximum acceptable milliseconds (integer-valued float).

unit.description: Unit of the threshold value. 'score' for 0.0-1.0 float axes; 'milliseconds' for latency.

aggregate_pass_policy.description: Policy for computing the aggregate pass/fail verdict from per-axis scores.

mode.description: How per-axis results are combined. 'all_axes_must_pass': every axis must individually pass its threshold. 'weighted_score_threshold': weighted sum of scores must exceed aggregate_threshold. Least-restrictive any-axis pass policies are intentionally rejected.

aggregate_threshold.description: Required only when mode == 'weighted_score_threshold'. The weighted-sum score must exceed this value to pass.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the aggregate pass policy choice and rationale. For weighted score mode, this must state that axis weights sum to 1.0 before the aggregate threshold is meaningful.
